rules: Builds go to candidate by default.
Direct builds into the stable output directory are rejected.
Stable is only replaced through explicit promotion.
Validators prefer the stable bundle unless an explicit product path is passed.

validatorPolicy.successRule: Success requires observed target value == expected text and product response invariants to hold; product status=succeeded alone is insufficient.

facts: Stable and candidate currently have the same bundle identifier.
Stable and candidate are ad-hoc signed.
Stable and candidate currently share the same CDHash.
Stable and candidate executables currently have the same SHA-1.
The stable bundle starts through the control wrapper.
The product reports accessibilityTrusted=false on the real stable bundle.
The product reports hotKey.registered=true on the real stable bundle.
Blocked hotkey responses exist on the stable bundle runtime.
The corresponding flow states are triggered -> inserting -> blocked.
Forced-trusted TextEdit and Safari runs reached product status=succeeded.
Those runs also reached flow state done and syntheticPastePosted=true.
During the same session, the validator observed targetValue='' and recorded target-value-mismatch for both runs.
The temporary validator result files from that earlier session are no longer present in /tmp, but the observation itself was direct.
A product-state artifact still reports running=true even though lastResponseStatus=stopped and the process is no longer active.

evidence: 2026-04-11 blocked rerun stderr: Blocked hotkey observation failed: Timed out: Condition not met within 10.0 seconds.
2026-04-11 TextEdit rerun stderr: TextEdit hotkey series failed: Timed out: Condition not met within 10.0 seconds.
2026-04-11 Safari rerun stderr: Safari hotkey series failed: Timed out: Condition not met within 20.0 seconds.
facts: These reruns do not count as new success evidence.

plausibleSchlussfolgerung: Accessibility trust should be treated as stable-bundle scoped, not candidate-build scoped.
Replacing the stable bundle with a different promoted candidate may require a fresh Accessibility grant.
The current ad-hoc signature and CDHash-bound designated requirement are the most plausible reason why rebuilds can invalidate trust.
The 2026-04-11 hotkey rerun timeout is more likely a validator-side trigger issue than proof of a product-flow regression.
nochOffen: Why the hotkey validator did not produce a new last-hotkey-response entry on 2026-04-11 despite a single running product instance.
Whether System Events based hotkey emission has become the fragile part of the validation path.
A deterministic fix for stale running=true status artifacts after stop.
Whether a fixed local signing identity should replace ad-hoc signing for longer-lived trust retention.

konkreterFolgeauftrag.title: Hotkey-Validator-Ausloesung und Statusartefakt final haerten
scope: Restore deterministic hotkey emission for the stable-bundle validator.
Re-run one real stable-bundle TextEdit success case and one Safari success case.
Confirm the new observedTargetValueMatches fields with fresh green evidence.
